news.headline: Petri Hofsté and Stuart Graham nominated by ING for Supervisory Board seats.
news.summary: Earlier today, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the United States had dropped to 4.2%, a decrease from the previous month's rate of 4.4%. This drop represents approximately 504,000 people who found employment during this time period. Additionally, the economy added 379,000 jobs in various sectors, surpassing the expected forecast by economists. The job gains were predominantly seen in the leisure and hospitality industries, with notable increases in the food services and drinking places sectors. The positive momentum in job creation has provided hope for economic recovery amidst ongoing challenges.

news.headline: Bitcoin rebounds 4% as Strategic Reserve disappoints, failing to meet market hopes following recent price drop.
news.summary: Following the disappointing Bitcoin Strategic Reserve executive order, Bitcoin bears saw a downturn in the BTC price. However, bullish investors have managed to bounce back, reclaiming the value. The market experienced a fluctuation with Bitcoin's price dropping initially, but it has since shown resilience. This recovery marks a positive trend in the market, showcasing the ongoing volatility in the digital currency sphere. Traders and investors are closely monitoring the situation as Bitcoin's price fluctuates around the (mention relevant price range) mark.

news.headline: Opus consortium wins 20-year vehicle inspection concession in Türkiye for all vehicles - including passenger cars, trucks, buses, motorcycles, and more.
news.summary: In Gothenburg, Sweden on March 7, 2025, the MOI Group consortium, led by Met-Gün and Opus Group, secured a 20-year contract to oversee the inspection of all types of vehicles, including passenger cars, trucks, buses, and motorcycles in Turkey. The concession, granted in late February, is a significant achievement for the consortium and covers a broad range of vehicles for inspection over the next two decades.

news.headline: Broadcom, Genesco, and 3 stocks to watch on Friday as U.S. stock futures rise. Algonquin Power expected to report earnings of 8 cents per share on revenue of $607 million, Genesco to post earnings at $3.31. Algonquin Power shares increased by 1.3% to $4.85 in after-hours trading.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures rose today, drawing investor focus to certain stocks: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (NYSE:AQN) expected to report quarterly earnings of 8 cents/share on $607.00 million revenue pre-market. Algonquin Power & Utilities shares rose by 1.3% to $4.85 in after-hours trading. Analysts anticipate Genesco Inc. (NYSE:GCO) to announce quarterly earnings of $3.31/share. For more details, visit a financial news website.

news.headline: Earnings report on March 7, 2025:- Creative Media: estimated $6.50 loss/share, $16.62M revenue- Lifeward: estimated $0.20 loss/share, $7.30M revenue- Mammoth Energy Services: estimated $0.02 loss/share, $46.70M revenue- BeOne Medicines: upcoming earnings report.
news.summary: Ahead of the market opening, several companies are poised to report their latest financial results. Creative Media (CMCT) is expected to disclose a quarterly loss of $6.50 per share with revenues around $16.62 million. Lifeward (LFWD) anticipates a quarterly loss of $0.20 per share on revenue of $7.30 million. Mammoth Energy Services (TUSK) is estimated to reveal a quarterly loss of $0.02 per share alongside revenue of $46.70 million. BeOne Medicines (ONC) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dow drops over 400 points due to retaliatory tariffs from China, Canada, and Mexico. US reports record trade deficit. Greed index remains in 'Extreme Fear' zone.
news.summary: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index was still in the "Extreme Fear" territory. Thursday saw U.S. stocks closing lower, with the Dow Jones index dropping over 400 points due to the implementation of U.S. tariffs on Canadian, Mexican, and Chinese imports and the subsequent retaliatory tariffs imposed by trade partners. Kroger Co. (NYSE:KR) exceeded expectations with its fourth-quarter earnings report. On the economic front, the U.S. recorded a record trade deficit of $131.4 billion in January, up from the previous month's $98.1 billion. Additionally, unit labor costs in the nonfarm business sector increased by 2.2%. Find more details on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Asana (ASAN) prepares for Q4 earnings release; analysts predict 1 cent/share loss, $188.13 million revenue, shares down 6.2%.
news.summary: On Monday, March 10, Asana, Inc. (NYSE:ASAN) will announce its fourth-quarter financial results after the market closes. Analysts predict the San Francisco-based company will post a quarterly loss of 1 cent per share, an improvement from the 4 cents per share loss reported a year ago. Asana is expected to reveal quarterly revenue of $188.13 million, up from $171.13 million in the previous year. Earlier, on Dec. 5, Asana surpassed third-quarter financial expectations and provided FY25 guidance that exceeded estimates. Following this news, Asana's stock price dropped by 6.2% to close at $17.78 on Thursday. For more detailed insights, readers can find the full story on Benzinga.com, complete with the latest analyst ratings and other relevant information.

news.headline: Polymarket CEO Invited to White House Crypto Summit, praised administration's support for innovators.
news.summary: In a significant development for Polymarket in the U.S., CEO Shayne Coplan confirmed he will attend the White House Digital Asset Roundtable. Coplan expressed gratitude to President Trump for the invitation, stating he is honored to represent Polymarket. He highlighted the administration's dedication to collaborating with American innovators, citing it as a revival of the American dream. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

AB DAO launches NewPay 1.9.0, urges 21,989 users to claim 65 million AB tokens, enhancing ecosystem.
news.summary: Singapore, March 7, 2025 - A major update, NewPay 1.9.0, has been officially launched by AB DAO, with the primary focus on enhancing the AB Ecosystem Wallet. The latest version brings significant improvements to the user experience and ecosystem functionality. In this update, the NEW tokens have been rebranded as AB tokens. This change is part of a wider effort to streamline operations and create a more cohesive ecosystem. The release marks a pivotal moment for AB DAO, with the company aiming to solidify its position in the market.

Costco CEO confident to mitigate Trump's tariffs impact with global buying power. One-third US sales from imports, less than half from tariff-impacted countries. Retailer aims to reduce price increases using supplier relationships. "Our goal is to lower prices first," says executive VP.
news.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp. disclosed a mixed second-quarter performance, recognizing the influence of tariffs on its operations. Despite this, the company remains optimistic about overcoming obstacles through its extensive global purchasing capabilities and supplier connections to minimize price hikes. Around 33% of Costco’s sales in the U.S. are generated from imports, with less than 50% originating from China, Mexico, and Canada, suggesting a considerable portion of their products may be impacted by tariffs. CEO Ron Vachris emphasized the company’s commitment to addressing this issue by utilizing their global buying power, robust supplier relationships, and fostering innovation. The company aims to be proactive in reducing prices when feasible and cautious in adjusting prices amidst escalating costs, as stated by Executive VP Gary Millerchip.

news.summary: On March 7, 2025, Baidu, Inc., a prominent AI company, disclosed its plan to raise up to $2 billion in total principal. The company's shares are listed on NASDAQ under BIDU and on HKEX as 9888 for the HKD Counter and as 89888 for the RMB Counter.

news.headline: BW LPG Limited announced an ex-dividend of US$0.42 on Oslo Stock Exchange today for Q4 2024. Trading ex-dividend starts today in Oslo and March 10, 2025 in NY Stock Exchange.
news.summary: In a recent press release, BW LPG Limited ("BW LPG") announced key details regarding the cash dividend for Q4 2024. The company, with OSE ticker code "BWLPG.OL" and NYSE ticker code "BWLP," stated that its shares will be traded ex-dividend on the Oslo Stock Exchange starting today and from 10 March 2025 on the New York Stock Exchange. BW LPG is a prominent player in the industry, being the foremost owner and operator of LPG vessels globally.

news.headline: Huawei presents healthcare solutions at MWC 2025, focusing on digital intelligence. Experts discuss digital transformation and AI in a global healthcare industry session.

The event, titled "Digital Intelligence Drives Inclusive Healthcare," featured experts discussing cutting-edge strategies and practices in digital transformation and AI. The session aimed to showcase innovations and best practices in leveraging technology to elevate healthcare accessibility and effectiveness.

news.headline: Biodegradable Plastic Additives Market set to reach $2.20 billion by 2029 with 8.1% CAGR growth, driven by focus on food safety and non-toxicity. Manufacturers aiming for growth in Asia Pacific. Major players include Cargill Inc., DIC Corporation, and Croda International Plc. Key drivers include government incentives, while challenges include competition from fossil-based alternatives. Limited supply chain remains a deterrent.
news.summary: The Biodegradable Plastic Additives Market is projected to increase in value from USD 1.49 billion in 2024 to USD 2.20 billion by 2029, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 8.1%. The market growth is attributed to the integration with bioplastics growth, biodegradability standards in single-use plastics, and a focus on food safety and non-toxicity. Manufacturers are targeting expansion in the Asia Pacific region, particularly in India and China. The market comprises key players such as Cargill Inc., DIC Corporation, DEKA Corporation, Croda International Plc, Stora Enso, UPM-Kymmene OYJ, Clariant AG, Borregaard AS, Corbion N.V., and Avient Corporation. Major drivers include a focus on food safety and non-toxicity, while a lack of established supply chains serves as a restraint. Government incentives and subsidies present opportunities, and competition from fossil-based alternatives poses a challenge. The bio-based non-aromatic segment holds the largest share in the market. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: European company Orphalan partners with SPH Kyuan Trade to enter China's Wilson Disease market. (Characters: 97)
news.summary: A recent collaboration with Kyuan Xinhai (Beijing) Medical Products Trade Co., Ltd, known as SPH Kyuan Trade, aims to revolutionize access to Wilson's disease treatment for patients in China. Orphalan, a leading company in the field of orphan drug innovation, announced this strategic partnership on March 7, 2025. The partnership seeks to enhance the availability of treatment options for individuals affected by Wilson's disease in China, with the goal of making a significant impact on healthcare in the region.
